summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the package root whose `sources` field uses GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) on line 6. npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — even without a declared install/postinstall script — and GYP evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell command during the configure step. This causes attacker-supplied shell to execute automatically on `npm install`. The package is described as an LDAP authentication plugin for Backstage and has no legitimate need for a native addon build, and the binding.gyp's only purpose appears to be to execute the embedded command rather than to compile real native sources. The content of the traced install path additionally tripped the upstream model-output safety filter, consistent with operational install-time malware shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
